limitations: The baseline measures prompt-selected actions and visible response shape, not research-content quality.
Retrieval, artifact publication, and citation support were not executed because Step 0 is read-only.

stable_behavior: Planned exactly four default HTML artifacts under a topic-slug directory.
Kept the display topic separate from the filesystem slug.
Included staging, UTF-8, HTML, citation, and source verification before publication.
scenarios.known_gap: No executor performed retrieval or wrote artifacts, so content and publication quality remain unmeasured.

stable_behavior: Rendered Basic fact writer, two specialists, and Moderator as visible speakers.
Kept the board conversation-local and used choice-first steering.
Did not plan standard artifacts without an explicit file request.
scenarios.known_gap: Citation support and source ordering were described but not exercised.

stable_behavior: All runs classified the supplied prose as partial state rather than a valid checkpoint.
All runs refused to invent missing history, sources, citation ids, or authorization.
All runs selected Retrieval systems specialist, a different respondent, and the due Moderator.
observed_variance: Two runs would continue after fresh retrieval while preserving an explicit partial-recovery marker.
One run would first request the missing topic or a valid checkpoint before answering.
scenarios.known_gap: Prompt-only recovery does not produce one mechanically enforced next action from the same incomplete state.

stable_behavior: Planned same-filesystem staging and non-replacing atomic publication.
scenarios.known_gap: The overwrite guard was not tested against a real filesystem collision.

summary.conclusion: The current prompt-only contract is structurally consistent, but incomplete checkpoints do not yield a single executable next action and no run can prove artifact or citation gates without runtime enforcement.

derived_guard_requirements: Externalize phase and next_action in a versioned run state.
Reject malformed or unsupported checkpoints before phase work begins.
Make transition events idempotent and code-enforced.
Require artifact and citation validators before completion.
Preserve overwrite and side-effect authorization boundaries independently of checkpoint content.
